_meta.benchmark_name: Phenobarbital Neonatal Population PK — Grasela & Donn 1985
_meta.source: Grasela TH Jr, Donn SM. Neonatal population pharmacokinetics of phenobarbital derived from routine clinical data. Dev Pharmacol Ther. 1985;8(6):374-83. PMID: 4075936
_meta.dataset: 59 preterm neonates with varying birthweights; multiple IV bolus doses; sparse sampling (1-3 observations/subject)
_meta.model: 1-compartment IV bolus, clearance scaled by birthweight (WT), proportional error
notes: Parameters normalised to a 1-kg neonate; multiply by actual weight to get individual parameter.
CL/kg and V/kg are the typical values from the published paper.
BSV expressed as approximate coefficient of variation (CV%) from the paper.
This dataset is available as MEMSS::Phenobarb in R and is Example 1 in NONMEM Users Guide Part V.
Half-life derived from CL and V: t½ = V*ln(2)/CL ≈ (0.96/0.0047)*0.693 ≈ 141 h.
OpenPKPD test uses a simulated dataset with identical population parameters (tests/external_validation/data/phenobarbital_simulated.csv).

derived.halflife_units: h (for 1 kg neonate at typical parameters)

dataset_citation: Grasela TH Jr, Donn SM (1985). Neonatal population pharmacokinetics of phenobarbital derived from routine clinical data. Dev Pharmacol Ther 8(6):374-383. [Simulated dataset (seed=42) generated from published population parameters: CL=0.0047 L/h/kg, V=0.96 L/kg, 59 preterm neonates.]
